lines.text: Ash on the rooftops again. The wells ran warm all week, and this morning the shaft-house door stood open.
Two loaves sat on the step — Marta's, still warm. Nobody watched you take up your packs. That is how Cinderfall says come back.

lines.speaker: Elder Bren
lines.text: The mountain's heart is stirring. Hear me out before you go down.

lines.speaker: Elder Bren
lines.text: The old crews sealed the vault at the third shaft when the mountain last woke. The key — cooled ember, cut by steadier hands than ours — was lost on the middle floor when the lift fell.

lines.speaker: Elder Bren
lines.text: Find it. Open the vault. Still the heart. The inn will keep your beds made. Cinderfall doesn't forget its own.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: I bake two extra now, mornings. Mind the burns down there — wax, ash-lily, patience. Come back and complain about my bread.

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.text: Beds are warm and cheap for shaft-crews. Sleep before you go down; the deep doesn't forgive tired.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Coldiron, plate, and what keeps you standing. Pay what's marked — I don't haggle with people saving my roof.
The mountain's heart cooled slowly, like a kettle taken off the fire. You came up with ash in your hair and the key still warm.
Marta's loaves were waiting at the shaft-house door. Nobody said much. The wells ran cool by morning.
